title: 1.1: Tests that processing a mail account with a mark-as-read rule triggers async task calls only for unseen messages
1.2: Tests that after processing a mark-as-read rule, previously unseen messages are now marked as seen
1.3: Tests that after processing a mark-as-read rule, the total number of messages in the mailbox remains the same
1.4: Tests that processing a mail account with a delete rule triggers async task calls for matching messages
1.5: Tests that after processing a delete rule, matching messages are removed from the mailbox
1.6: Tests that processing a mail account with a flag rule only consumes unflagged messages
1.7: Tests that after processing a flag rule, previously unflagged messages are now flagged
1.8: Tests that after processing a flag rule, the total number of messages in the mailbox remains the same
1.9: Tests that after processing a move rule, matching messages are removed from the original mailbox folder
1.10: Tests that after processing a move rule, matching messages appear in the destination folder
1.11: Tests that a subject filter narrows consumed messages to only those with matching subjects when processing a mail account
1.12: Tests that a body filter narrows consumed messages to only those with matching body content when processing a mail account
1.13: Tests that a sender (from) filter narrows consumed messages to only those from matching senders when processing a mail account
1.14: Tests that combining multiple filter types together narrows the consumed message set to only those matching all active filters
2.1: Places the test for mark-as-read mail rule processing in paperless_mail/tests/
2.2: Places the test for delete mail rule processing in paperless_mail/tests/
2.3: Places the test for flag mail rule processing in paperless_mail/tests/
2.4: Places the test for move mail rule processing in paperless_mail/tests/
2.5: Places the test for filter combinations in paperless_mail/tests/
3.1: All tests use the Django TestCase framework as the base test class
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern test_<name>
3.3: All tests create model instances (MailAccount, MailRule) using Django ORM objects.create() method
3.4: All tests invoke the mail processing pipeline through handle_mail_account() rather than calling handle_mail_rule() directly
3.5: All tests use self.assert* style assertions (e.g., self.assertEqual, self.assertTrue)
4.1: The filter combinations test resets the mailbox state between each filter scenario to ensure independent evaluation of each filter type
4.2: The filter combinations test isolates each filter scenario so that filter criteria do not carry over between scenarios
